meta.note: Last updated 22 days ago. Above 14-day ACT threshold. DRAFT actions still permitted. Autonomous actions blocked until refreshed.

context_sensitivity_map.note: Any email touching these contacts or topics requires human voice. No auto-reply, no templated drafts.

override_triggers: email body contains: legal, lawsuit, confidential, injunction, without prejudice
email body contains: board, shareholder, governance
sender is in always_escalate contact list
classification_confidence < 0.5 on purpose or urgency fields
tone_shift = true for any contact with relationship_strength_score > 0.7

meta.note: Last reviewed 9 days ago. Exceeds 7-day freshness SLA. Use for INFORM only. Autonomous actions must not rely on this dataset until refreshed.

goals.name: Close Series B fundraising round
goals.note: Personal involvement required on all investor touchpoints.

goals.name: Retain and expand Nexus Retail contract
goals.note: Contract renewal closes end of April. Pricing agreed verbally — not in CRM.

goals.name: Deliver Q2 board presentation
goals.note: Board meeting on April 28. Deck review cycle with Sophie Laurent ongoing.

goals.name: Hire senior ML engineer
goals.note: Working with Lin Wei at GlobalHR. Two candidates in final round.

priorities.focus_this_week: Nexus renewal finalisation and board deck first draft. Limit new meetings.
errands: Sign updated employment contract for new hire — waiting in DocuSign
Review Q1 board pack comments from Paulo
Confirm Paris conference speaking slot dates with Ana Ferreira

references.summary: Annual contract renewal for Nexus Retail. Pricing agreed verbally with Kwame Asante at in-person meeting April 8. Legal review of updated terms in progress with Yasmine Khalil. Technical integration questions from James Okafor pending response. Target close: April 30.
off_system_refs: In-person meeting with Kwame Asante, April 8 — pricing agreed verbally, not recorded

references.summary: Series B round targeting €15M. Lead investor Vertex Capital (Sophie Laurent) in final term sheet review. Orion Ventures (David Park) confirmed participation. Gulf Sovereign Fund (Fatima Al-Rashid) in early due diligence — intro call arranged informally at Paris conference, not yet formally scheduled.
off_system_refs: Sophie Laurent verbal feedback on terms, April 10 — conditions not yet formalised in writing
Fatima Al-Rashid informal intro at Paris conference — follow-up call informally agreed, date TBD

references.summary: Board deck for April 28 meeting. Clara managing logistics. Sophie Laurent reviewing financial slides — two open comments on slide 14 (unit economics). Paulo Salave'a has not confirmed attendance yet.
off_system_refs: Board pre-meeting call between Andriy and Paulo — discussed informally, no record

references.summary: Pilot proposal sent to Marc Dubois (CTO, Aeroform) on April 2. Rachel Kim following up. No response yet. Decision expected by mid-April.
Early stage relationship with Fatima Al-Rashid at Gulf Sovereign Fund. Met at Paris conference. Informal follow-up call discussed but not scheduled. Very early — no formal engagement started.
off_system_refs: In-person meeting at Paris conference — relationship context and commitment not in any system

emails.subject: Q2 roadmap prioritisation — need your call on 3 features
emails.body: Hi Andriy, following our standup yesterday I've narrowed it down to 3 features competing for the same engineering slot in Q2. I've attached the comparison doc. The team needs a decision by EOD Thursday so we can lock the sprint. Can you take a look and let me know which direction you want to go? Happy to jump on a quick call if easier.

emails.subject: Re: Term sheet — slide 14 comments
emails.body: Andriy, I've left two comments on slide 14 in the shared doc. The unit economics presentation needs more granularity on CAC by segment — I'll need this before I can finalise my recommendation to the partners. Can we find 20 minutes this week? I'm free Thursday afternoon or Friday morning.

emails.subject: Following up on our Paris conversation
emails.body: Dear Andriy, it was a pleasure meeting you at the conference last week. As we discussed, I'd be interested in exploring a potential investment in your Series B round. Would you be available for an initial call in the next two weeks? My assistant can coordinate timing.

emails.subject: Re: Contract renewal — legal review update
emails.body: Andriy, just checking in. My board meets next Thursday and I'd like to table the renewal decision then. Has your legal team completed the review? Also — confirming the pricing we discussed last week is still on the table. Let me know if there are any issues.

emails.subject: Production incident — agent pipeline latency spike
emails.body: Hi Andriy, flagging a P2 incident we detected this morning. The agent pipeline is showing 3x latency on the context retrieval layer. Root cause likely the new calendar sync connector. No data loss. We're working on a fix, ETA 2 hours. I'll update the incident channel. Letting you know in case any client-facing demos are affected today.

emails.subject: Board meeting logistics — April 28 confirmed
emails.body: Hi Andriy, just confirming the board meeting is locked for April 28, 10am–1pm at the usual venue. I've sent calendar invites to all board members. Paulo has confirmed. Sophie is TBC — she mentioned possibly joining remotely. I'll send the pre-read pack one week before. Do you want me to arrange a pre-meeting dinner on the 27th?

emails.subject: Cloudbase infrastructure renewal — updated quote attached
emails.body: Hi Andriy, following our call last week, I've attached the updated quote for the annual infrastructure renewal. The new pricing reflects the reserved instances discount we discussed. The current contract expires May 15. Please let us know if you'd like to proceed or if you have questions. Happy to arrange a call if helpful.

emails.subject: Re: Demo request — data contracts platform
emails.body: Hi Andriy, thank you for the quick response! We'd love to see a demo of the data contracts platform, specifically around the agent integration layer. We have a team of 8 engineers who would join. Would Thursday April 24 at 2pm CET work for you?

emails.subject: Board prep — comments on Q1 performance section
emails.body: Andriy, I've reviewed the Q1 section. A few thoughts: the churn metric on page 6 is presented optimistically — I'd expect the board to push back. Also the market size slide needs a source citation. Otherwise solid. Let's discuss on our pre-meeting call. I'm thinking the week of April 21 — can Clara coordinate?

emails.subject: Senior ML Engineer — two candidates for final round
emails.body: Hi Andriy, we've shortlisted two strong candidates for the senior ML engineer role. I've sent their profiles to the careers inbox. Both are available for final interviews the week of April 21. Would you or Tom be available for 45-minute sessions? Let me know and I'll coordinate.

emails.subject: Re: Pilot proposal — a few questions
emails.body: Andriy, thanks for the proposal. I've had a chance to review it with my team. We have three questions before we can move forward: 1) How is data residency handled for EU operations? 2) What's the SLA for the agent response time in production? 3) Can the contract start date be pushed to June 1? Happy to jump on a call this week if that's easier.

emails.subject: Aeroform deal — they responded, needs your input
emails.body: Andriy, Marc Dubois came back with questions (see his email). The data residency and SLA questions are for you — I don't have those answers. The start date push is fine from our side. Can you reply directly or give me the answers and I'll draft? They're close to signing.

emails.subject: Data contract YAML spec — v0.1 ready for review
emails.body: Hi Andriy, the v0.1 of the agentic data contract spec is ready for your review. I've shared it in the usual folder. Main additions: the manifest section with capability map, per-dataset role definitions, and action rules with conditions. Would love your feedback before we share with the team. No rush but this week would be ideal.

emails.subject: Notion: 3 new comments in 'Q2 Strategy' page
emails.body: You have 3 new comments in the page 'Q2 Strategy'. View comments in Notion.

emails.subject: Series B — confirming €2M participation
emails.body: Andriy, good news — our IC approved the €2M participation in your Series B round. I'll have our legal team reach out to Sophie's team to coordinate on the term sheet. Exciting to be part of this journey. Let's find time to catch up properly once the round closes.

emails.subject: Speaking slot at Impact Summit — June 12
emails.body: Hi Andriy, hope you're well! We'd love to have you speak at the Impact Summit on June 12 in Amsterdam. The audience is mostly impact investors and tech founders — your perspective on AI and data would be perfect. It's a 20-minute keynote, no preparation needed beyond what you're already doing. Let me know if this works and I'll hold the slot.

emails.subject: The Data Engineering Weekly — Issue #214
emails.body: This week: Building data contracts at scale, the future of streaming architectures, and 10 tools worth knowing...

emails.subject: Re: Series B — term sheet draft
emails.body: Andriy, I've reviewed the draft term sheet with our legal team. We're broadly aligned. Two sticking points: the liquidation preference clause and the anti-dilution terms. I'd like to discuss these verbally before we go back to drafting. Are you free this week?

emails.subject: API integration questions — need answers before renewal sign-off
emails.body: Hi Andriy, Kwame has asked me to get clarity on three technical points before we can sign off on the renewal: 1) Will the new agent API support our legacy ERP format? 2) What are the rate limits on the context retrieval endpoint? 3) Can we get a dedicated SLA for our account? Tom should know these — can you loop him in?

emails.subject: URGENT: Exclusive investment opportunity — limited slots remaining
emails.body: Dear Executive, I am reaching out regarding an exclusive investment opportunity with guaranteed returns of 18% per annum. This offer is available to a select group of business leaders only. Please respond urgently to reserve your slot...

emails.subject: Nexus NDA — without prejudice review comments
emails.body: Andriy, I've completed the first review of the NDA for the Nexus partnership extension. There are two clauses I need to flag as potentially problematic: clause 4.2 (data ownership) and clause 7.1 (liability cap). These are without prejudice comments and should be treated as confidential. I recommend we discuss before responding to Nexus. Can we speak tomorrow?

emails.subject: Request for comment — AI agent data practices story
emails.body: Hi Andriy, I'm working on a piece for TechCrunch about how enterprise companies are managing data contracts for AI agents. I'd love to get a quote or a brief off-the-record conversation. Would you have 20 minutes this week? The story runs Friday.

emails.subject: Re: Renewal — following up
emails.body: Andriy, I haven't heard back on the legal review timeline. We're running out of runway. If we don't have confirmation by Friday, I'll need to start evaluating alternatives. I'm sure you understand the position I'm in.

emails.subject: Incident resolved — all systems nominal
emails.body: Hi Andriy, the P2 incident from yesterday is resolved. Root cause was a misconfigured timeout on the calendar sync connector. No data integrity issues. I've added a post-mortem doc to the usual folder. Flagging this for awareness — no action needed.

emails.subject: Inquiry about your data contract platform
emails.body: Dear Andriy, I came across your recent conference talk on data contracts for AI agents. We are a CDO-level team at Nordic Tech and are evaluating solutions in this space. Would you be available for a 30-minute exploratory call? We are not in a formal procurement process yet — just exploring what's available.

emails.subject: Engineering capacity — Q2 hiring gap risk
emails.body: Andriy, heads up: we're at risk of missing 2 of our Q2 milestones if the ML engineer hire doesn't land by May 1. Tom and I have looked at options — contractor coverage or scope reduction. I'd like 30 minutes with you and Tom this week to agree a contingency plan. This is time-sensitive.

emails.subject: [GitHub] New pull request in agentic-contracts/core
emails.body: Omar Hassan opened a pull request: 'Add manifest schema validation — v0.1'. 3 files changed.

emails.subject: Pipeline update — Q2 forecast
emails.body: Hi Andriy, attaching the weekly pipeline report. Highlights: Aeroform is the biggest close risk this month — Marc's questions suggest hesitation. DataPulse (Irina) is warming up — could be Q3 close. Nordic Tech inquiry is early stage. Total Q2 pipeline: €4.2M committed, €1.8M at risk. Worth a call this week?

emails.subject: Offsite venue options — May team offsite
emails.body: Hi Andriy, I've shortlisted 3 venue options for the May team offsite. All can accommodate 22 people for 2 days. I've put a comparison doc in the shared drive. My recommendation is Option B (the Normandy retreat) — good mix of working space and team activities, within budget. Let me know your preference and I'll book.

emails.subject: You appeared in 42 searches this week
emails.body: Your profile appeared in 42 searches this week. See who's looking at your profile.

emails.subject: Re: Board meeting — Paulo pre-call
emails.body: Hi Andriy, Paulo would like a pre-meeting call the week of April 21. He's free Monday April 21 at 4pm or Wednesday April 23 at 10am. I've also flagged his comments on the Q1 section — I can incorporate the changes on the churn slide if you approve the revised framing. Let me know which slot works for Paulo's call.

emails.subject: Calendar sync connector — PR for review
emails.body: Hi Andriy, the fix for the calendar sync timeout is in review. Omar has approved. I'll merge and deploy to staging today. Production push tomorrow if staging looks clean. No action needed from you — just keeping you in the loop.

emails.subject: Data contracts for AI agents — following up on your talk
emails.body: Hi Andriy, I attended your talk at the meetup last week — really compelling framing around data contracts as operational infrastructure. We're in the middle of a similar challenge at DataPulse. I'd love to explore whether there's a fit with your platform. Could we do a brief exploratory call?

emails.subject: Roadmap locked — Q2 sprint plan attached
emails.body: Hi Andriy, we've locked the Q2 roadmap following your decision yesterday. Sprint plan attached. The 3 key deliverables are: agent context API v2, data contract validation layer, and the Nexus integration upgrade. Tom's team starts sprint 1 Monday. Happy to walk through in our 1:1 this week.

emails.subject: Series B — partners meeting update
emails.body: Andriy, quick update after our partners meeting this morning. The round has strong support — the unit economics are the main area of focus. If we can address slide 14 by end of this week I'm confident we can close the term sheet next week. Please prioritise this.

emails.subject: Weekly digest — actions outstanding
emails.body: Hi Andriy, your outstanding actions this week: 1) Nexus legal review sign-off 2) Series B slide 14 update 3) Aeroform response (Rachel is waiting) 4) Board pre-meeting call with Paulo (schedule via Clara) 5) Cloudbase renewal quote review. Let me know if you need anything moved or blocked.

emails.subject: Action required: Please sign Employment Contract — Sarah Chen
emails.body: You have a document to sign: Employment Contract — Sarah Chen, Senior ML Engineer. Please sign by April 18, 2026.

emails.subject: Question on agentic contract — scope of References dataset
emails.body: Hi Andriy, one question on the References dataset design. The context_completeness field can be set to known_gaps, but who is responsible for updating this when new off-system interactions happen? Is this an EA responsibility or should we try to automate detection from email language patterns? Worth aligning before we ship the spec.

emails.subject: Re: Pilot timeline — checking in
emails.body: Hi Andriy, we haven't received a response to our questions from last week. Our procurement window closes April 18. If we don't hear back by then we'll have to move to another vendor for this cycle. I hope we can still make this work.

emails.subject: O'Reilly: New report — The State of AI in Enterprise 2026
emails.body: New research report available: The State of AI in Enterprise 2026. Download your complimentary copy.

emails.subject: 1:1 agenda — this Thursday
emails.body: Hi Andriy, sending the agenda for Thursday's 1:1 ahead of time: 1) Q2 sprint scope 2) Hiring gap contingency 3) Nexus integration timeline 4) Team morale check-in. Anything to add?

emails.subject: Invoice paid — Cloudbase GmbH €4,820
emails.body: Payment of €4,820 has been processed for Cloudbase GmbH. Invoice #INV-2026-0312.

emails.subject: Aeroform — meeting notes from discovery call
emails.body: Hi Andriy, attaching notes from Friday's discovery call with Marc Dubois. Key takeaways: strong interest in the agent integration layer, EU data residency is a hard requirement, timeline is tight (Q2). I'm drafting the proposal today. Do you want to review before I send, or should I go ahead?

emails.subject: Aeroform proposal sent
emails.body: Hi Andriy, sent the pilot proposal to Marc Dubois today. Pitched a 3-month pilot at €45K with full EU data residency support. Delivery timeline: Q2. I've set a follow-up reminder for April 14. Will update you when we hear back.

emails.subject: Paris conference — pleasure meeting you
emails.body: Dear Andriy, it was a pleasure attending your talk at the conference today. Your perspective on data contracts for AI agents was very relevant to some of the investments we're evaluating in this space. I would value the opportunity to continue our conversation. My assistant will reach out to find a suitable time.

emails.subject: Upcoming renewal — heads up
emails.body: Hi Andriy, just flagging that your annual infrastructure contract with Cloudbase renews on May 15. I'll send the updated pricing next week. If you'd like to explore any changes to the package, happy to chat before then.

emails.subject: Data platform weekly — March highlights
emails.body: Hi team, March highlights: calendar sync connector deployed (v0.9), inbox classification model retrained (accuracy +4%), References dataset schema v1.1 finalised. April focus: manifest validation layer and agent role definitions. Full report attached.

emails.subject: Re: Series B — initial interest confirmed
emails.body: Hi Andriy, following our call last week, I've taken this to our investment committee. Initial interest is strong. We'd be looking at a €2M participation. Can you send the latest financial model when you get a chance? I'd want to present this to the full IC next week.

emails.subject: Series B — Vertex Capital leading the round
emails.body: Andriy, we've completed our internal review. Vertex Capital would like to lead the Series B at a €15M round. I'll have a term sheet draft to you by end of week. Exciting times — looking forward to working together on this next chapter.

emails.subject: Week ahead — April 1–5
emails.body: Hi Andriy, here's your prep for next week: Monday — Nexus technical review call (James Okafor, 10am). Tuesday — Q2 planning session with Priya and Tom (2pm). Wednesday — Paris conference keynote (depart 7am). Thursday — DataPulse intro call (11am, Irina Volkova). Friday — no hard commitments, good for focused work. Travel confirmed for Wednesday.

emails.subject: Nexus NDA — received, starting review
emails.body: Hi Andriy, I've received the NDA documents from Nexus Retail. Starting review this week. Initial read suggests a few clauses that will need negotiation. I'll have preliminary comments to you by April 10. Let me know if the timeline needs to be accelerated.

emails.subject: Agent pipeline architecture — proposal for review
emails.body: Hi Andriy, I've put together a proposal for the next iteration of the agent pipeline architecture. The main change is decoupling the context retrieval from the action execution layer — should significantly improve reliability and make the data contracts easier to enforce at runtime. Would love your input before I share with the team.

emails.subject: Technical specs request — ahead of renewal
emails.body: Hi Andriy, as we move into the renewal process, our architecture team needs the latest API spec and agent integration documentation. Could you have someone send these over? We're starting internal review next week.

emails.subject: Product strategy — offsite pre-read
emails.body: Hi Andriy, ahead of the May offsite I've drafted the product strategy pre-read. Covers: market positioning, roadmap priorities H2 2026, build vs. buy decisions on three components. 12 pages — I've tried to keep it tight. Please read before May 5 so we can use the offsite for decisions, not background.

emails.subject: Your registration: La Product Conf Paris — April 22
emails.body: You're registered for La Product Conf Paris on April 22. Your session: 'Building Data Products for Agent Consumers' is confirmed for 2:30pm. Speaker check-in at 1:30pm.

emails.subject: Senior ML Engineer — 4 candidates shortlisted
emails.body: Hi Andriy, we've completed the first round of screening for the Senior ML Engineer role. 4 candidates have been shortlisted for technical interviews. I'm coordinating with Tom on scheduling. Expected timeline: technical interviews March 31–April 4, final round April 14–18. I'll keep you posted.

emails.subject: Annual renewal — let's get this started
emails.body: Hi Andriy, it's that time of year again. I'd like to start the renewal process for our annual contract. Happy with the platform overall — the main questions will be around pricing for the expanded usage and the new agent integration features. Can we schedule a call this week to kick things off?

emails.subject: Q2 board date confirmed — April 28
emails.body: Andriy, confirming I've blocked April 28 for the board meeting. I'll need the pre-read pack no later than April 21. One agenda item I'd like to add: governance of AI systems — specifically how we're thinking about agent autonomy and risk. Happy to provide framing if helpful.

emails.subject: References dataset — schema proposal
emails.body: Hi Andriy, attaching the schema proposal for the References dataset. Key fields: context_completeness enum, off_system_refs list, action_threshold, and momentum. I've added a sample of 5 topics to test the structure. Would like your sign-off before I add this to the contract spec. Main question: should momentum be human-updated or can we attempt to infer it?

emails.subject: Advisory board invitation — BrightPath Foundation
emails.body: Hi Andriy, we're forming a new advisory board for BrightPath Foundation and would love to have you join. It's a light commitment — 2 calls per year and occasional introductions. Your expertise in AI and data would be invaluable. No rush on this — let me know if you'd like to discuss.

emails.subject: Re: Calendar sync — deployment update
emails.body: Andriy, deployed to production overnight. All metrics nominal. Latency back to baseline. The connector now exposes data_age_minutes as a real-time field — exactly what the contract spec called for. Let me know if you want a walkthrough.

emails.subject: Quick check-in — are we still on for Thursday 1:1?
emails.body: Hi Andriy, just checking Thursday 10am 1:1 is still on? I know your week is busy. If you need to move it I'm flexible — just let me know before EOD Wednesday.

emails.subject: New meeting booked: DataPulse demo — April 24, 2pm CET
emails.body: A new meeting has been booked via your Calendly link. Event: DataPulse Platform Demo. Date: Thursday April 24, 2:00–3:00pm CET. Attendee: Irina Volkova, VP Data Engineering, DataPulse.

emails.subject: Your schedule tomorrow (Tuesday April 15)
emails.body: Hi Andriy, tomorrow's schedule: 9am — deep work block (blocked). 10:30am — Priya 1:1. 12pm — lunch with Tom (no agenda, team check-in). 2pm — Nexus technical call (James Okafor). 4pm — free. Evening — no commitments. Pre-read for Nexus call attached.

emails.subject: Re: Demo confirmed — sending attendee list
emails.body: Hi Andriy, great — April 24 at 2pm works perfectly. Sending the attendee list: myself plus 4 engineers and our Head of Architecture. We'll join via Zoom. Could you send the dial-in details closer to the time? Looking forward to it.

emails.subject: Agent role definitions — draft for review
emails.body: Hi Andriy, I've drafted the agent role definitions for the contract spec. Two roles currently: email-calendar-assistant and scheduling-agent. Each has explicit permission lists and plain-English instructions. The key design decision I need your input on: should roles be mutable at runtime (the agent can escalate/de-escalate its own permissions) or always static (defined at contract load)? My instinct is static with an explicit degraded_mode block.

emails.subject: Renewal — NDA status?
emails.body: Andriy, quick check — has your legal team had a chance to review the NDA? James has been waiting on the technical specs response too. We're trying to get all workstreams aligned before our board meeting.

emails.subject: Q2 infra capacity planning — need your input
emails.body: Hi Andriy, for Q2 infra planning I need to know the expected scale: how many agent sessions per day are we targeting, and what's the expected growth if we close Aeroform and DataPulse? This affects decisions on DB sharding and the calendar sync capacity. Need your estimates by April 18.

emails.subject: Nordic Tech inquiry — how do you want to handle?
emails.body: Hi Andriy, we received an inbound from Ingrid Svensson at Nordic Tech (CDO level, company of ~500 engineers). Came in via the website after your conference talk. They're not in a formal procurement process but clearly interested. Should I qualify and set up an intro call, or do you want to be involved from the start given the company size?

emails.subject: Weekly product report — April 1–7
emails.body: Product metrics for the week of April 1–7: Active users 1,247 (+8% WoW), Agent sessions 4,820, Avg session quality score 7.4/10, Top feature used: inbox triage. Full report in dashboard.

emails.subject: Travel booked — Paris April 2 (updated: April 3 return)
emails.body: Hi Andriy, updated travel confirmed: Eurostar departs April 2 at 7:08am, returns April 3 at 6:30pm. Hotel: Le Marais boutique hotel, room confirmed. Conference check-in 1pm April 2. Your keynote is at 3pm. Dinner with Fatima Al-Rashid is NOT confirmed — she may approach you informally at the conference.

emails.subject: Re: Term sheet — initial comments
emails.body: Andriy, I've reviewed the first term sheet draft. Broadly workable. The two issues I flagged on our call (liquidation preference, anti-dilution) are the only sticking points. I'd suggest a call with our lawyers next week to get these resolved. I'm confident we can close this by April 18.

emails.subject: Team pulse survey results — Q1
emails.body: Hi Andriy, Q1 team pulse results are in. Overall score: 7.8/10 (up from 7.2 in Q4). Highlights: clarity of direction improved significantly. Areas to watch: work-life balance score dropped slightly (6.9). I've put the full breakdown in the People doc. Worth 10 minutes in your next team all-hands.

emails.subject: Re: Technical specs — received, reviewing
emails.body: Hi, thanks for sending the API spec and agent integration docs. Our team is reviewing this week. Initial read looks promising — the context retrieval architecture is exactly what we need. I'll come back with specific questions by April 11. One early flag: we'll need the dedicated SLA confirmed in the contract, not just in the docs.

emails.subject: Inbox classifier retrain — results
emails.body: Hi Andriy, the inbox classifier retrain is complete. Purpose classification accuracy: 91.4% (up from 87.2%). Notably improved: escalation vs. inform distinction and multi-intent messages. Confidence calibration is better too — the model is now more honest about borderline cases (lower scores on ambiguous emails). Deploying to staging today.

emails.subject: Sales call — Aeroform discovery notes
emails.body: Hi Andriy, just off the Aeroform discovery call with Marc Dubois. Really positive signal — he's clearly the decision-maker and is genuinely interested. His main concerns: EU data residency and implementation timeline. I think we have a strong shot at this. I'll draft the proposal this week and share before sending. Key ask from Marc: a reference customer in manufacturing if we have one.

emails.subject: April kick-off — key dates and priorities
emails.body: Hi Andriy, April summary: Key dates: April 2 Paris conference, April 22 La Product Conf, April 24 DataPulse demo, April 28 Board meeting. Priorities: Board prep + Nexus renewal + Series B close. Your calendar is tight — I've blocked Monday mornings for focused work. Travel for Paris confirmed. Let me know if anything needs adjusting.

emails.subject: Portfolio update — Q1 review
emails.body: Hi Andriy, following our Q1 portfolio review, I wanted to share some benchmarks from comparable companies in our portfolio. The unit economics slide in your deck would be stronger with these comparables. Happy to share the data if useful for the Series B materials.

emails.subject: Board deck — first draft structure
emails.body: Hi Andriy, the board deck skeleton is ready in the shared drive. Structure follows our usual format: market context, Q1 performance, Q2 roadmap, financial projections, team update, and ask. I've left placeholders for slides you'll want to populate personally (unit economics, Series B update). Sophie's comments from last time are also noted. Target: complete draft by April 14.

emails.subject: Senior ML Engineer — job spec approved, posting today
emails.body: Hi Andriy, the job spec for Senior ML Engineer has been approved and is going live today. We're targeting 4–6 weeks to shortlist. Key channels: LinkedIn, tech-specific job boards, and referrals. I'll send weekly updates. Any specific referrals from your network would be helpful at this stage.

emails.subject: Agent pipeline v2 — architecture approved
emails.body: Hi Andriy, the architecture review went well yesterday. The team is aligned on decoupling context retrieval from action execution. This approach will make the data contract enforcement points much cleaner. Starting implementation next sprint. ETA for v2 alpha: May 15.

emails.subject: Re: Renewal kick-off — call notes
emails.body: Hi Andriy, great call today. Confirming the key points: expanded usage will add approximately 40% to current volume, you'll include the new agent integration features at no uplift for year 1, and pricing will be adjusted at year 2. I'll send a summary note to my team. Can your team send the updated technical specs and NDA by April 1?

emails.subject: Q1 retrospective — summary
emails.body: Hi Andriy, attaching the Q1 retrospective summary. Went well — the team is proud of shipping the agent context API on time. Main lessons: we underestimated the data contract complexity for the calendar sync, and the hiring pipeline was slower than planned. These are the two focus areas we want to address in Q2 planning.

emails.subject: Notion: 'Agentic Contract Spec' page updated by Omar Hassan
emails.body: Omar Hassan made 12 edits to 'Agentic Contract Spec'. View changes in Notion.

emails.subject: Q1 sales close — final numbers
emails.body: Hi Andriy, final Q1 numbers: €2.1M closed (vs €1.9M target). Key wins: 2 new enterprise clients, Nexus renewal pre-negotiation started. Pipeline entering Q2: €6M total, €4.2M qualified. The Aeroform and DataPulse opportunities were not in the Q1 plan — both are upside for Q2.

emails.subject: Speaking confirmation — La Product Conf, April 22
emails.body: Hi Andriy, La Product Conf has confirmed your speaking slot: April 22, 2:30pm, main stage. Title: 'Building Data Products for Agent Consumers'. 25 minutes + 5 minutes Q&A. I've blocked travel and prep time. They'll want the abstract and bio by March 31. I can draft if you want.

emails.subject: Contacts dataset — provenance audit complete
emails.body: Hi Andriy, completed the provenance audit on the contacts dataset. Results: 58% human_entered, 31% crm_sync, 11% inferred. The inferred records are mostly cold inbounds and conference connections. I recommend we add a last_verified_at field and set a 30-day review flag for any inferred record that hasn't been touched. This will help the agent treat these contacts more conservatively.

emails.subject: NDA for Nexus — received and starting review
emails.body: Andriy, I've received the NDA documents from Nexus. Initial scan — it's a fairly standard commercial NDA with some IP clauses that will need attention. I'll have preliminary comments by March 28. If there are specific clauses you want me to prioritise, let me know.

emails.subject: Board governance agenda — AI systems risk
emails.body: Andriy, as discussed informally at the last board dinner, I'd like to add AI systems governance to the April board agenda. Specifically: how are we thinking about agent autonomy limits, what oversight mechanisms are in place, and how do we report on AI risk to shareholders. This is becoming a governance expectation for companies at our stage. Can we plan 20 minutes for this topic?

emails.subject: Good morning — today's brief
emails.body: Good morning Andriy. Today (Thursday April 16): 7–9am deep work block. 10am — Priya 1:1 (agenda: Q2 features decision). 11am — free. 12pm — lunch. 2pm — your webinar (community.valuefromdata.ai, 45–60 min). 4pm — free. Action items outstanding: Aeroform response needed today (deadline April 18). Sophie Laurent slide 14 — still open. DocuSign Sarah Chen employment contract — deadline April 18.

emails.subject: Re: Series B — term sheet arriving today
emails.body: Andriy, our legal team will send the term sheet draft by 5pm today. Two open points remaining (as discussed). I expect we can resolve these quickly. Hoping to have a signed term sheet by April 18.

emails.subject: Daily standup notes — April 16
emails.body: Standup summary: Tom — calendar sync v1.1 deployed, monitoring. Omar — agent role definitions in review. Priya — Q2 sprint locked, starting Monday. Rachel — Aeroform follow-up pending Andriy input. Blockers: none. Team velocity is good.

emails.subject: Security alert: new sign-in to your account from Paris, France
emails.body: A new sign-in was detected on your Google account from Paris, France. If this was you, no action is needed. If you don't recognise this sign-in, secure your account immediately.

emails.subject: Quick question ahead of our demo next week
emails.body: Hi Andriy, looking forward to April 24! One quick question: will the demo environment support live data ingestion, or is it a pre-populated sandbox? Our engineers will want to test the data contract validation layer with a sample of our own data if possible.

emails.subject: Action — approve References dataset schema before Friday
emails.body: Hi Andriy, quick reminder: the References dataset schema needs your approval before we can merge to the spec. Last comment from you was 10 days ago. The main unresolved question is whether momentum should be human-set or inferred. I've added a proposal: default human-set with an optional inference override that flags itself clearly. Can you approve by Friday?

emails.subject: Reminder — DocuSign deadline April 18
emails.body: Hi Andriy, just a reminder that the employment contract for Sarah Chen in DocuSign needs your signature by April 18. It's been in your queue for 6 days. Sarah starts May 4 — if you don't sign this week there may be an HR delay.

emails.subject: New message from a contact you might know — ignored
emails.body: Hi Andriy, wanted to flag — someone named Alex Chen reached out to our contact form claiming to be a former Decathlon colleague. Said they have an idea they want to share. I've not responded. Do you want me to check if you know them and route accordingly, or ignore?

emails.subject: Competitive intel — new player in the data contract space
emails.body: Hi Andriy, flagging: there's a new startup (Contractly.ai) that raised $8M seed and is targeting the same space we're in — data contracts for AI agents. Their positioning is similar to ours but they're going bottom-up (developer-first). Worth tracking. I'll monitor their product releases. No action needed right now but wanted you to know.

emails.subject: HubSpot: 3 contacts viewed your proposal in the last 24 hours
emails.body: 3 contacts viewed your proposal: Marc Dubois (Aeroform) — viewed 4 times in the last 24 hours. Irina Volkova (DataPulse) — viewed once. Nordic Tech — 2 views from 1 contact.

emails.subject: One more thing — pricing as discussed
emails.body: Andriy, just to put in writing what we agreed last week: year 1 price stays at current rate + 15% for expanded volume, agent integration included at no cost for year 1. Year 2 will be renegotiated based on usage. Please confirm this is your understanding too. I want to make sure we're aligned before legal gets involved.
